informal: If $\varphi$ is a homomorphism of $G$ onto $G'$ and $N \triangleleft G$, show that $\varphi(N) \triangleleft G'$.
lean:
theorem Herstein_exercise_2_7_7_corrected {G : Type*} [Group G] {G' : Type*} [Group G']
    (φ : G →* G') (hφ : Function.Surjective φ) (N : Subgroup G) [N.Normal] :
    (Subgroup.map φ N).Normal
reasoning: The informal statement says φ is a homomorphism of G onto G' (i.e., surjective) and N is normal in G, then φ(N) is normal in G'. The Lean signature has φ : G →* G' (group homomorphism), hφ : Function.Surjective φ (onto), N : Subgroup G with [N.Normal] instance, and concludes (Subgroup.map φ N).Normal, which is exactly the image φ(N) being normal in G'. The hypotheses, conclusion, and types all align precisely.
